title: Modal Dialog
subtitle: Overlay modal dialogs via CDK Overlay with full focus management, animations, minimization, and theme integration

basic.title: Basic Usage
basic.description: Opens a modal from the ModalDialogService. The modal slides in from the edge of the screen with a smooth animation.

semanticTypes.title: Semantic Types
semanticTypes.description: Use semantic helpers to quickly open pre-styled dialogs.
semanticTypes.note: You can launch a state modal with openInfo(), openWarning(), openSuccess(), and openError().

verification.title: Verification Dialog
verification.description: Requires the user to type a specific phrase before confirming a destructive action.
verification.note: Use openVerification() and set verificationText, verificationLabel, and verificationErrorMessage in the configuration to specify the verification text, input field label, and error message displayed when the text does not match.

loader.title: Loading Modal
loader.description: Displays a non-closable loading spinner during long-running operations.
loader.note: Use openLoader() and close the reference when the operation is complete.

dynamicComponent.title: Dynamic Component
dynamicComponent.description: Opens a modal with a custom Angular component as the body.
dynamicComponent.note: Inject MODAL_DIALOG_DATA to receive data and MODAL_DIALOG_REF to control the modal from within.

minimizable.title: Minimizable Modal
minimizable.description: Allows users to minimize the modal to a chip in the bottom dock without losing context.
minimizable.note: A unique id is required when minimizable: true. Calling open() with the same id will restore the minimized modal instead of creating a new one.

timeout.title: Timeout with Progress Bar
timeout.description: Automatically closes the modal after a configurable duration. A visual progress bar tracks the remaining time and can be paused on mouse hover.
timeout.note: Use the timeout option in the modal configuration to enable this feature. You can set duration in seconds, showProgressBar to display the progress bar, pauseOnHover to pause the timer on mouse hover, and timeoutAction to execute a custom function when the time is up ('confirm' | 'cancel').

statusBar.title: Status Bar
statusBar.description: Adds a colored border strip on one side of the modal to convey additional semantic information using the statusBar option in the configuration.

footer-custom.title: Custom Actions
footer-custom.description: Customize the modal actions with custom buttons
footer-custom.note:
You can use the customButtons option in the modal configuration to define buttons with specific actions or nuiModalDialogFooter for full footer customization via an ng-template.

To use loading or disabled reactively, you can inject the ModalDialogActionsService into your child component to control the button states from within the component, use the nuiModalDialogAction directive on your custom buttons to bind them to predefined actions like 'confirm' or 'cancel', or emit custom events and subscribe to them from the child component or using an ng-template, without a directive, from the parent component itself.

child-footer-actions.title: Actions from Child Component
child-footer-actions.description: Customize the modal actions from a dynamic component loaded inside the modal.
child-footer-actions.note: Inject the ModalDialogActionsService into your dynamic component to add buttons to the modal footer from within the component.
child-footer-actions.note2: As an alternative, use the nuiModalDialogFooter directive inside a dynamic component for full control of the footer with native HTML. Unlike ModalDialogActionsService, which registers button data rendered by the modal shell, the directive projects a complete ng-template: any Angular bindings are automatically re-evaluated on each change detection cycle, without needing to call update().

html-content.title: HTML Content
html-content.description: Add HTML content to your message.
html-content.note: Use message for static markup or server-side rendering. The string is sanitized by Angular's DomSanitizer.

body-template.title: Body Template
body-template.description: Pass an Angular TemplateRef via contentTemplate to use Angular bindings inside the modal body. Combine it with templateContext to inject dynamic data.
body-template.note: The template is rendered with *ngTemplateOutlet. Use let-name="key" in the template to access values from the templateContext.

prevent-close.title: Prevent Close
prevent-close.description: Use preventClose to intercept close attempts and block them conditionally. Supports synchronous and asynchronous guards. Subscribe to closePrevented() to react when a close is blocked.
prevent-close.note: The preventClose function must return true (allow) or false / Promise<false> (block). It runs on every close attempt: ESC, backdrop click, and the X button.

backdrop.title: Backdrop and Close Triggers
backdrop.description: Control the visibility of the backdrop and which user actions can close the modal using hasBackdrop, backdropClass, closeOnBackdropClick, and closeOnEscape.
backdrop.note: Setting hasBackdrop: false completely removes the overlay, allowing interaction with the page behind the modal.

update-ref.title: Hot Updates via ModalDialogRef
update-ref.description: Use ref.updateTitle(), ref.updateHeaderTemplate(), and ref.updateFooterTemplate() to update an open modal without closing and reopening it.
update-ref.note: Title updates are reactive via signals and render synchronously, even when called outside the Angular zone (e.g., from setTimeout).

service.title: ModalDialogService
service.description: Inject ModalDialogService to open and manage modals. All methods return a ModalDialogRef.

config.title: ModalDialogConfig
config.description: Configuration object passed to open(). Properties are merged with the global defaults from provideNUI().

ref.title: ModalDialogRef
ref.description: The reference returned by open(). Use it to close the modal, listen to events, and observe lifecycle changes.

tokens.title: Injection tokens
tokens.description: Use these tokens to inject modal context inside a dynamic component loaded by the service.

custom-button.title: ModalDialogCustomButton
custom-button.description: Inline button definition passed via customButtons in the config. Evaluated once at open time — use ModalDialogActionsService for reactive states.

modal-action.title: ModalDialogAction
modal-action.description: Footer action registered from a dynamic component via ModalDialogActionsService. Supports reactive disabled and loading via getter functions.

actions-service.title: ModalDialogActionsService
actions-service.description: Injected by the dynamic component to register footer actions programmatically. A fresh instance is created per modal by ModalDialogService.

dock-tab-config.title: dockTabConfig
dock-tab-config.description: Customization of the chip shown in the NuiDock when the modal is minimized. Properties are mapped to the NuiDockItem managed by NuiDockService.

footer-directive.title: ModalDialogFooterDirective
footer-directive.description: Directive applied to an ng-template inside the dynamic component. Projects the full template into the modal footer with native Angular bindings.

colors.title: Color tokens
colors.description: Override these CSS custom properties to adjust modal dialog colors for your brand or custom theme.

layout.title: Layout tokens
layout.description: Control dimensions, spacing, typography, and layer (z-index) of the modal dialog via these CSS variables.

dock.title: NuiDock tokens
dock.description: Customize the appearance of the unified dock for minimized items (modals and sidebar panels). The --nui-dock-* tokens control the chips; use --nui-dock-z-index and --nui-dock-offset for positioning.

keys.title: Translation tokens
keys.description: The Modal Dialog component uses its own tokens and some shared tokens.
keys.note: All user-facing text in Modal Dialog can be overridden globally using provideNuiI18n(). Others can be translated by passing the custom translation in the open() configuration.

roles.title: ARIA roles and attributes
roles.description: The modal shell automatically applies the required ARIA attributes for screen reader compatibility.

focus.title: Focus management
focus.description: The CDK FocusTrap ensures keyboard navigation is confined to the modal while it is open.
focus.note: Focus is automatically restored to the element that triggered the modal when it closes.

options.title: Global config options
options.description: Default values applied to every modal opened with ModalDialogService. Override per-modal by passing config to open().

example.title: Usage example
example.description: Configure global defaults in your app.config.ts using provideNUI().
